title: Fix issue with order of flags
body:
The order of the flags was casuing issue.
Enabled SkipArgReorder to fix the problem.

Signed-off-by: umohnani8 <umohnani@redhat.com>

comments.body: This looks fine to me, but is there an issue or an explanation of what the problem you were seeing?
I would like to see a test that will prevent regression.

@rhatdan the issue was raised by QE in https://trello.com/c/WHprWjvV/744-1-cri-okpod-add-dir-transport-to-kpod-save.
This is what was happening:
```
 ➜  libpod git:(stats) sudo podman save --compress --format oci-dir -o blah alpine     
option --format requires a value

➜  libpod git:(stats) sudo podman save --format oci-dir --compress -o blah alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:04a094fe844e055828cb2d64ead6bd3eb4257e7c7b5d1e2af0da89fa20472cf4
4.20 MB / 4.20 MB [========================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:501d1a8f0487e93128df34ea349795bc324d5e0c0d5112e08386a9dfaff620be
584 B / 584 B [============================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
```
The order of the flags was causing issues, enabling SkipArgReorder fixed it.
Added test, it passes for now but will fail if a regression occurs.
